Title: All product images broken for problem_user
Description: When logged in as problem_user all 6 product images show the same broken 404 placeholder instead of correct product images.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Go to saucedemo.com / 2. Login as problem_user with password secret_sauce / 3. Observe product images on inventory page
Expected Result: Each of the 6 products displays its own unique and correctly loaded image
Actual Result: All 6 products display the same broken placeholder image sl-404.jpg
Impact: Visual defect - product catalog is unusable for this user type

Title: Login performance degradation for performance_glitch_user
Description: Login introduces an approx. 7 second artificial delay before the inventory page loads. Standard user loads in under 1 second.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Go to saucedemo.com / 2. Login as performance_glitch_user with password secret_sauce / 3. Measure time until inventory page loads
Actual Result: Inventory page took 7143ms - approximately 7x slower than standard_user
Impact: Performance regression - poor user experience on login

Title: Checkout flow accessible with empty cart
Description: The Checkout button is active when the cart has zero items. No validation or warning is shown and the user is taken directly to the checkout form.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Login as standard_user / 2. Go to cart page with no items / 3. Click Checkout
Expected Result: App should block checkout and show: Your cart is empty. Add items before checking out.
Actual Result: User is redirected to checkout-step-one.html and can complete the full checkout flow with no items
Impact: In a real application this creates a zero-item order impacting order management and reporting

Title: Cancel on checkout overview returns to inventory not cart
Description: Cancelling at the Overview step redirects to the inventory page instead of returning to the cart. Items remain in the cart but navigation is counterintuitive.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Add item to cart / 2. Proceed to checkout step 1 and click Continue / 3. On the Overview page click Cancel
Expected Result: Cancel at Step 2 should return user to the cart page
Actual Result: User is redirected to /inventory.html and must manually navigate back to cart
Impact: UX inconsistency - may be intentional design decision

Title: Cart QTY field is not editable
Description: The QTY column in the cart shows the value 1 as static text. Users cannot change quantity and must return to inventory to add items multiple times.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Add any item to cart / 2. Go to cart page / 3. Try to edit the QTY value
Expected Result: QTY should be editable to allow increasing quantity from the cart
Actual Result: QTY is rendered as static text and cannot be changed
Impact: Known limitation of the demo app - in production quantity editing would be expected
